title: Stable Subarray Reversal with Incorrect Shifts

statement.markdown: You are given an array of integers and a shift value. Your task is to detect whether every subarray of the given array, shifted to the right or left by the given shift value, is stable or not. A subarray is considered stable if it can be sorted by reversing a part of it. For example, in the array [4, 3, 2, 1] shifted by 1, the subarray [3, 2, 1] is stable because it can be sorted by reversing the first two elements. However, the subarray [4, 3, 2] is not stable. Your task is to write a function that determines whether every subarray, when shifted by the given value, is stable or not.
statement.inputFormat: n is the number of elements in the array and shift is the shift value. The input will be in the format n, followed by n integers, and a single integer shift.
statement.outputFormat: a boolean value indicating whether every subarray, when shifted by the given value, is stable or not.
statement.notes: You may assume that the array contains only integers, and that the shift value is non-negative. You may also assume that the array is not empty.

starterCode.java: // TODO: implement the solve function to return a boolean indicating whether every subarray when shifted by the given value is stable or not

functionSignature.java: public boolean solve(int[] nums, int shift);
